description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed from horizontally stacked, dark wooden logs, giving it a robust and traditional appearance. The roof is a simple, steeply pitched gable style, covered in weathered wooden planks, and features a prominent stone chimney emerging near one end. The front facade includes a rectangular doorway and a single window, while the longer sides each have two small, rectangular windows set symmetrically. The logs are visibly textured, with rounded ends at the corners, emphasizing the handcrafted construction. There is no visible porch or decorative trim, and the overall design is utilitarian, suited for rural or forested environments. The cabin's interior is likely hollow, providing space for occupancy.
description: A dark, weathered log cabin with a gable roof, stone chimney, and small rectangular windows. The structure is simple and rustic, with visible log textures and no porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gable roof, central stone chimney, and two small windows on the facade.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the main entrance door and a single window, with the roof sloping down and the chimney visible at the far end.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying two small, evenly spaced windows and the continuation of the log construction.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with no windows or doors, highlighting the log ends and the roof's pitch.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with a steep pitch, constructed from wooden planks and featuring a stone chimney
attribute_window_arrangement: Four small, rectangular windows: two symmetrically placed on each long side, and one on the front facade next to the door. All windows are single and evenly spaced.
attribute_door_placement: One exterior door located on the front facade, positioned to the right of the single front window.
